summary: Native callsite-register recovery now recognizes CMOVcc register and memory sources. The session/open and session/close builders feed 0x14062BCE0 through conditional stack/local selections rather than plain MOV writes, so the previously unknown submitter URL/body arguments are now mapped as conditional-memory-load values.

tooling_update.change: CMOVcc destination recovery reports conditional-register-copy or conditional-memory-load, records the condition suffix, preserves stack-local offsets for RSP/RBP memory sources, and resolves origins for conditional register copies.

calling_convention.RDX: composed endpoint URL string selected through CMOVNZ
calling_convention.R8: flattened payload/body string selected through CMOVNZ

server_implications: OpenNXT should model session/open and session/close as request-builder endpoints submitted through the same async transport boundary as the OTL/auth paths.

The client can conditionally pass empty or prior-buffer values for URL/body based on the CMOVNZ guard state, so server-side parity tests should not assume every session request is built by one linear string append path.
session/close carries user hash and session duration variants, giving the server a concrete endpoint family to stub or log during 947 auth/session parity work.
next: Trace the CMOVNZ predicate setup immediately before 0x14062AAE0 and 0x14062AE37 to name the empty/body-present branch condition.
Fold the session/open and session/close endpoint contracts into OpenNXT logging or local endpoint stubs once the surrounding request object lifetime is named.
